The '''MK-series maintenance droid''' was a [[Maintenance droid/Legends|repair droid]] manufactured by [[Kalibac Industries]].

==Characteristics==
The MK-series was designed be an assistant and occasionally the only mechanic on-board a [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. It had tracked locomotion and an advanced [[Heuristic processor/Legends|heuristic processor]]. Kalibac engineers wanted the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] to be able to handle all kinds of situations it might encounter, even new or unexpected ones. They consulted with a wide range of starship [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]], [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanics]] and even possibly [[Outlaw tech/Legends|outlaw techs]] to make the droid as close as possible to an organic mechanic.<ref name="SGD" />

While this made the droid very capable of finding creative solutions to problems, it also led it to developing a few personality quirks. The MK-series tended to be aggressive and reckless, since they were very confident in their abilities to repair themselves and their ships.<ref name="SGD" />

A MK-series droid cost 11,080 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] new.<ref name="SGD" />

==History==
[[File:Marko.jpg|left|thumb|[[MK-09/Legends|MK-09]], a particular MK-series droid belonging to the [[Azzameen family]].]]
[[MK-09/Legends|MK-09]], otherwise better known as Emkay, was of the MK-series.

==Behind the scenes==
MK-09's droid series was first identified in a [[LucasArts]] website detailing portions of the then-recently released ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' as the "MK-09 maintenance droid". Since then, ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' altered the series name to the "MK-series maintenance droid".
